subject: Joint Distributions
question: The joint pdf of $X$ and $Y$ is given by $f_{XY}(x,y) = \begin{cases} 4e^{-2(x+y)} & 0 < x < \infty,\ 0 < y < \infty \\ 0 & \text{Otherwise.} \end{cases}$ Are $X$ and $Y$ independent?
explanation: Marginal density $f_X(x)$ is given by $f_X(x) = \int_0^{\infty} 4e^{-2(x+y)}\,dy = 2e^{-2x} \int_0^{\infty} 2e^{-2y}\,dy = 2e^{-2x}$, $x > 0$. Similarly, the marginal density $f_Y(y)$ is given by $f_Y(y) = \int_0^{\infty} 4e^{-2(x+y)}\,dx = 2e^{-2y} \int_0^{\infty} 2e^{-2x}\,dx = 2e^{-2y}$, $y > 0$. Now since $f_{XY}(x,y) = 4e^{-2(x+y)} = [2e^{-2x}][2e^{-2y}] = f_X(x)f_Y(y)$, $X$ and $Y$ are independent
